title: Ptilagrostiella kingii
taxonomy: Ptilagrostiella kingii is classified within the family Poaceae, which is commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Ptilagrostiella is further classified within the tribe Stipeae. Ptilagrostiella kingii is a species of grass that is endemic to Mexico. It is a rare and poorly known species within its genus.

intro: Ptilagrostiella kingii, also known as King's gama grass, is a type of plant species that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues and infections. Bioactive constituents of Ptilagrostiella kingii include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
